Use a Trie (Prefix Tree) to store words.
For `addWord`, traverse the string and create Trie nodes for each character.

For `search`, you must navigate through the Trie. When encountering a regular character, move to that child node. When encountering a `.` (dot), use Depth-First Search (DFS) or recursion to try reading the sub-word starting from *every* populated child node.
Since `.` matches anything, a `.` at the current node implies any of the 26 child characters might be the correct path. Check them all until one returns `true` or all fail.
